mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals impending corporate revenue contraction, triggering risk-off repricing and negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in industrial production and consumer price inflation indicates supply-constrained demand that compresses real corporate margins, depressing equity returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally when both INDPRO and CPIAUCSL rise concurrently for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts coupled with expanding consumer credit indicate robust aggregate demand, supporting earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.05 when HOUST and RSAFS both increase month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained payroll contraction signals weakening corporate hiring demand and impending earnings downgrades, leading to negative equity pressure within 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls below 155000 for two consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when PAYEMS declines for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between accelerating real GDP growth and rising federal funds rates creates a policy tightening shock that overvalues equities, forcing a mean-reversion decline over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: overheating economy with hawkish central bank
failure_condition: Walk-forward correlation between the GDPC1-FEDFUNDS divergence signal and SPX returns is non-negative over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent expansion in industrial production reflects healthy capacity utilization and corporate profitability, driving positive equity returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no positive correlation with INDPRO trends, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment breaching 4.5% acts as a recession threshold, triggering algorithmic risk management and equity sell-offs within the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Markets remain range-bound or rise when UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or forward returns are not statistically lower than baseline (p > 0.05).
